notes: Placeholder for a reviewed county current permit source. A reviewer must provide and approve the authoritative REST endpoint before AutoMap treats this as current permit data.
limitations: No verified URL is configured yet. Do not use as an official permit layer.

notes: Placeholder for a reviewed county current planning case source.
limitations: No verified URL is configured yet. Coverage, case status, date fields, and authority must be reviewed.

notes: Real Cabarrus REST layer discovered from the Current_Accela_Permits/Plan_Reviews service. Use only as plan-review/development-pipeline signal pending staff review.
limitations: Proxy/context only. It must not be treated as official completed development, entitlement approval, permit issuance, final permit issuance, or final capacity.

notes: Candidate source bucket for subdivisions, current projects, active plan reviews, or other early development activity signals.
limitations: Proxy/context only unless an authoritative pipeline source is supplied and approved.

notes: Real Concord REST planning case layer discovered from Planning_Webmap_Public_MIL1. Candidate only because coverage appears municipal and requires staff confirmation.
limitations: Coverage limited to Concord only unless verified otherwise. Do not use for Kannapolis, Harrisburg, Midland, Mount Pleasant, Locust, or countywide requests unless coverage is confirmed.

notes: Real NCDOT hosted ArcGIS REST layer for 2024 AADT station context. Approved for transportation reference only.
limitations: Reference/context only. AADT is traffic context and is not a development pipeline, permit, entitlement, or capacity approval source.

notes: Real NCDOT ArcGIS REST MapServer for 2026-2035 STIP points and lines. Approved for transportation project reference only.
limitations: Reference/context only. STIP project context is not development approval or parcel suitability by itself.

notes: Future candidate bucket for utility context layers if a reviewed source is supplied.
limitations: Proxy/context only. Must not be interpreted as official utility capacity.
